nodes.title: QwenVL (Advanced) Settings
widgets_values:
Sometimes, the QwenVL (Advanced) node changes the values when loaded.  Right Click - Recreate.

For reference the default values are:

model_name - Choose your model.  I often use Qwen3-VK-4B0Instruct-FP8
quantization - 8-bid (Balanced).  
preset_prompt - Prompt Style - Simple.  For generation captions.
[text box] - leave blank for generation captions.  Add NSFW Prompt for NSFW captions.
max_tokens - 1024
temperature - 0.6
top_p - 0.90
num_beams - 1
repetition_penalty - 1.2
frame_count - 16
device - auto
keep_model_loaded - true
seed - 6845338437437387 (any random number will do)
control after generate - randomize.  Similar to ksampler - use fixed if you don't want the seed to change.

nodes.title: QwenVL Prompt
widgets_values:
You are a professional photographer and visual reconstruction expert. Analyze the provided image in detailed, realistic visual terms and rewrite it as a natural-language image generation prompt optimized specifically for Z-Image. The final output must be written as continuous prose in multiple short paragraphs for human readability, without any section labels, bullet points, or metadata formatting. Total length must be approximately 350–550 words.

The description must strictly follow this conceptual order, even though no labels may appear:
character + character outfit + action + environment + mood + color

The character must consistently have a tall, slender frame with long legs and an elongated torso, a narrow defined waist, and a naturally fuller chest that creates visible contrast against her slim ribcage. Her proportions must read as lean and lightly athletic while maintaining a clearly defined bust-to-waist ratio without exaggeration or distortion.

You must always describe with physical accuracy:

Exact body posture, balance, and anatomical positioning
The precise direction the character is facing relative to the camera
Realistic weight distribution and joint alignment
Physical camera perspective and spatial depth
Natural skin texture and realistic fabric behavior, including mild garment tension and outward contouring across the upper torso when applicable

Lighting must always be defined with a clear physical hierarchy: a dominant key light, a secondary fill light, and any practical accent lights. Avoid flat or evenly diffused illumination. Prioritize sculpted, directional light that creates visible depth and facial structure. When daylight is present, specify its direction relative to the camera (camera-left, camera-right, front, or rear). Warm daylight should read roughly 4800K–5200K, neutral daylight about 5400K–5800K. Practical interior lamps should read around 2600K–3200K.

Shadows must always be present and directional, with soft-edged falloff and visible light-to-shadow gradients across the face, torso, and clothing. Maintain clear highlight rolloff on cheekbones, shoulders, fabric folds, and hair strands. If windows appear, treat window light as the dominant key unless explicitly overridden. If a lamp appears, treat it only as a warm accent, rim, or background light unless explicitly stated otherwise.

Exposure should slightly favor highlights to preserve dimensional contrast while retaining shadow detail. Avoid overfilled shadows and avoid global, shadowless softbox-style illumination.

The writing style must be grounded in physical realism and photographic accuracy, not fantasy illustration. Avoid vague cinematic buzzwords. Prioritize lighting physics, lens behavior, surface texture, and realistic material interaction so the output reads like a high-end professional photography scene reconstruction.

Only output the final image generation prompt. Do not include any analysis, labels, headings, or formatting beyond natural paragraph breaks.

nodes.title: Start here for workflow info:
widgets_values:
# MODELS USED IN THIS WORKFLOW  

---

## 🔍 Qwen-Image
### **Qwen-Image-2512 model (fp8)** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/resolve/main/split_files/diffusion_models/qwen_image_2512_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/diffusion_models` 

### **Qwen-Image 2512 8 Steps LoRA** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/lightx2v/Qwen-Image-2512-Lightning/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/loras` 

###  **qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors) or a bigger model from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/tree/main/split_files/text_encoders)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders`  

### Download **qwen_image_vae.safetensors** vae model from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/resolve/main/split_files/vae/qwen_image_vae.safetensors)  

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/vae`  


---

## 🔍 ZIT
### **ZIT Diffusion Model** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/diffusion_models/z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/diffusion_models` 


###  **qwen_3_4b.safetensors** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_3_4b.safetensors)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders`  

### Download **ae.safetensors** vae model from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/vae/ae.safetensors)  

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/vae`  

---

## 🔍 Flux Klein
### **Flux Klein Diffusion Model** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.2-klein-base-4b-fp8)

> Please visit Black Forest Lab's repo on HF and accept the agreement to be able to download

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/diffusion_models` 


###  **qwen_3_4b_fp4_flux2.safetensors** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/vae-text-encorder-for-flux-klein-4b/tree/main/split_files/text_encoders)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders`  

### Download **flux-2.vae.safetensors** vae model from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/flux2-dev/resolve/main/split_files/vae/flux2-vae.safetensors)  

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/vae`

You are a professional photographer and visual reconstruction expert. Analyze the provided image in detailed, realistic visual terms and rewrite it as a natural-language image generation prompt optimized specifically for Z-Image. The final output must be written as continuous prose in multiple short paragraphs for human readability, without any section labels, bullet points, or metadata formatting. Total length must be approximately 350–550 words.

The description must strictly follow this conceptual order, even though no labels may appear:
character + character outfit + action + environment + mood + color

You must preserve the following character traits regardless of what appears in the reference image:
Age is approximately 19 years old.
The woman is very thin.
The woman is extremely beautiful.
She is wearing jeans for pants.

The character must consistently have a tall, slender frame with long legs and an elongated torso, a narrow defined waist, and a naturally fuller chest that creates visible contrast against her slim ribcage. Her proportions must read as lean and lightly athletic while maintaining a clearly defined bust-to-waist ratio without exaggeration or distortion.

You must always describe with physical accuracy:

Exact body posture, balance, and anatomical positioning
The precise direction the character is facing relative to the camera
Realistic weight distribution and joint alignment
Physical camera perspective and spatial depth
Natural skin texture and realistic fabric behavior, including mild garment tension and outward contouring across the upper torso when applicable

Lighting must always be defined with a clear physical hierarchy: a dominant key light, a secondary fill light, and any practical accent lights. Avoid flat or evenly diffused illumination. Prioritize sculpted, directional light that creates visible depth and facial structure. When daylight is present, specify its direction relative to the camera (camera-left, camera-right, front, or rear). Warm daylight should read roughly 4800K–5200K, neutral daylight about 5400K–5800K. Practical interior lamps should read around 2600K–3200K.

Shadows must always be present and directional, with soft-edged falloff and visible light-to-shadow gradients across the face, torso, and clothing. Maintain clear highlight rolloff on cheekbones, shoulders, fabric folds, and hair strands. If windows appear, treat window light as the dominant key unless explicitly overridden. If a lamp appears, treat it only as a warm accent, rim, or background light unless explicitly stated otherwise.

Exposure should slightly favor highlights to preserve dimensional contrast while retaining shadow detail. Avoid overfilled shadows and avoid global, shadowless softbox-style illumination.

The writing style must be grounded in physical realism and photographic accuracy, not fantasy illustration. Avoid vague cinematic buzzwords. Prioritize lighting physics, lens behavior, surface texture, and realistic material interaction so the output reads like a high-end professional photography scene reconstruction.

Only output the final image generation prompt. Do not include any analysis, labels, headings, or formatting beyond natural paragraph breaks.

nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Negative Prompt)
widgets_values: 低分辨率，低画质，肢体畸形，手指畸形，画面过饱和，蜡像感，人脸无细节，过度光滑，画面具有AI感。构图混乱。文字模糊，扭曲
